name: plugin lifecycle: completed migration writes lock.json.migrations_applied:[{from, to, applied_at}] so subsequent installs can detect already-migrated state
command: mkdir -p .claude-tdd-pro && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/rubric/lock.sh" --init && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/migrate.sh" --from 0.3.0 --to 0.4.0 && grep -E "migrations_applied" .claude-tdd-pro/lock.json 1>&2 && grep -E "0.3.0" .claude-tdd-pro/lock.json 1>&2 && echo ok 1>&2
